question: Design N-to-2^N Binary Decoder
description:
Implement a parameterizable binary decoder converting N-bit input to 2^N one-hot output with enable control.

' +
        '**Example (N=3):**
' +
        '```
input=5, enable=1 → output=0b00100000 (bit 5 high)
input=3, enable=0 → output=0b00000000
```

' +
        '**Constraints:**
' +
        '- Parameterizable N
' +
        '- Output is one-hot when enabled, all-zero when disabled
' +
        '- Exactly one output bit high when enabled
requirements: PARAMETERIZATION: Parameter N defines input width. Output width is 2^N.
DECODING LOGIC: For input value i (0 to 2^N-1), output[i]=1 and all other outputs=0.
ENABLE CONTROL: Input enable signal. When enable=1, perform decoding. When enable=0, all outputs=0.
ONE-HOT OUTPUT: Exactly one output bit is 1 when enabled. Verify one-hot property.
EDGE CASES: Handle N=1 (2 outputs), large N values, enable transitions.
TEST CASE 1 - Decode Input Zero: enable=1, in=0. Expected: one-hot output bit0.
TEST CASE 2 - Decode Input Three: enable=1, in=3. Expected: one-hot output bit3.
TEST CASE 3 - Decode Input Seven: enable=1, in=7. Expected: one-hot output bit7.
TEST CASE 4 - Disabled All Zero: enable=0. Expected: output all zeros regardless of in.
TEST CASE 5 - Full One-Hot Sweep: enable=1 for in=0..7. Expected: each output equals 8'b1 shifted by index.
TEST CASE 6 - Enable Toggle: Same in with enable high then low. Expected: active decode then all zeros.
